parsed_review.summary: CP00-660 (RP21 Admin Console failure recovery fixture transition descriptor, risk A, 10 units, RP21.P07.M05.S21-RP21.P07.M06.S08) passes hardened read-only review with no findings. The descriptor-only production-ready claim is supported by consistent implementation, validation, and tests: registry binding, contract projection (cp660_descriptor/requirements/no_write_attestation/hermes+claude packets/closeout_handoff), service builders (service.js:4209-4376), the CP660 descriptor validator enforcing ~31 false-flags, no-leak guards, and runtime-closed boundaries (validators.js:2813), and model tests asserting coverage, descriptor-only rows, runtime closure, and no-write attestation (model.test.js:1170-1230). The validator script regenerates the projection and deepEquals the committed contract to source, asserting the CP659->CP660->CP661 handoff chain (next_subphase RP21.P07.M06.S09). Deliverable distribution (claude_review 1, implementation 1, failure_recovery 7, security_audit 1) and micro-title split (Permission And Audit Binding 2, Synthetic Fixture Set 8) match across baseline, manifest, and contract. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced at code, validator, and test levels with all runtime flags false and runtime_opening_pack_id CP00-661; no secrets or real data present. Authority boundary is correct: Claude review packet is read_only with blocks_pack_on_p0_p1_p2, Hermes packet emits no runtime receipt and opens no runtime (evidence-label only, not product runtime). Manifest honestly reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=pending_review pending exactly one valid review (this one). CP178+ hardening satisfied: readiness auth preflight returned valid non-empty JSON, forbidden_review_evidence list present, and no invalid prior review attempts. Pre-review command evidence is all exit_code 0. The pack does not claim runtime or enterprise trust, so this CP closeout goal is not blocked.
